description:
Advances: specled.branch_guard

Deliverable:
Scope-expansion ticket, carved by the orchestrator after specled_-s65.4's implementer diagnosed a genuine specled_ex bug blocking its own merge gate.

Root cause (independently confirmed by the orchestrator, file:line refs verified):
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex:121 computes the branch report's status as
  `"status" => if(findings == [], do: "pass", else: "fail")` — it ignores
  finding severity entirely. ANY finding, including a lone `:info`-severity
  finding, marks the branch report "fail".
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex:107 does `if branch_report["status"] == "fail" do`
  to decide whether `mix spec.check` exits non-zero. So an info-only finding
  makes the whole command exit 1.
- This directly contradicts .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md's own scenarios,
  e.g. `specled.branch_guard.scenario.file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades`
  (~line 220-233): "a branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding fires at
  severity `info`" + "mix spec.check exits zero" — the spec already promises
  this behavior; it is unimplemented.
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex:93-95 already implements the correct pattern for
  the OTHER report (SpecLedEx.verify/3's status): `errors = count(severity ==
  "error")`, `warnings = count(severity == "warning")`, `fail? = errors > 0 or
  (strict? and warnings > 0)`. branch_check.ex should mirror this severity-aware
  approach instead of its current finding-count check.
- Confirmed empirically: specled_-s65.4's implementer applied the blessed
  `Spec-Drift: test_only` trailer (downgrades branch_guard_missing_subject_update
  to `:info` per lib/specled_ex/branch_check/trailer.ex), and `mix spec.check`
  still exited 1 with only the info-severity finding present.
- The existing unit test test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs (~line 449,
  `file_touch_yields_to_attested_file` test) only asserts the finding's
  `severity` field equals `"info"` — it never asserts the report's `status`
  field or an end-to-end exit code, which is how this shipped unnoticed.

Fix:
- In lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, replace the finding-count status check
  with a severity-aware one, following the verifier.ex convention: count
  `severity == "error"` findings; the branch report fails only when that count
  is nonzero (branch_check has no `strict?` flag today — before deciding
  whether `:warning`-severity findings should also fail, read every scenario
  in .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md and .spec/specs/spec_drift_trailer.spec.md
  that names a `:warning`-severity finding and check whether any asserts
  "mix spec.check exits non-zero" for a warning-only case, e.g. via
  `.spec/config.yml`'s `branch_guard_untested_realization: warning` default —
  if such a scenario exists, warnings must also fail (do not silently change
  established behavior for that code); if no such scenario exists, only
  `:error`-severity findings should fail.
- Update or add a test that asserts the end-to-end `status`/exit behavior for
  an info-only finding (mirroring the exact case in
  `file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades`) — not just the finding's severity
  field. This is the assertion gap that let the original bug ship.
- If a spec-verification stub needs flipping from `execute: false` to
  `execute: true` to cover this end-to-end behavior, do so in
  .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md; do not otherwise edit spec prose beyond
  what's needed to keep the existing scenarios accurate.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: the updated/added test(s) in test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
  asserting severity-aware status computation (info-only -> status "pass",
  error-present -> status "fail", and the warning case per whatever the spec
  scenarios require).
- Behaviors to demonstrate: a branch with only an info-severity
  branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding (e.g. via a `Spec-Drift: test_only`
  trailer) makes `mix spec.check` exit 0, matching
  `specled.branch_guard.scenario.file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades`.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check/trailer.ex
- test/test_helper.exs
- .spec/config.yml
- .spec/specs/spec_drift_trailer.spec.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not change how findings are generated (severity resolution, trailer
  parsing) — only how the aggregate report status/exit code is derived from
  already-computed severities.
- Do not silently change exit behavior for any severity that an existing
  scenario says must fail (read every relevant scenario before deciding
  warning-severity behavior; do not guess).
- Do not touch test/test_helper.exs — that belongs to the sibling ticket
  specled_-s65.4, which is blocked on this fix.
- Conventions: escape interpolation in doc examples; @impl with explicit
  behaviour module; to_timeout/1 not :timer helpers.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-b086eeaf
Log: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-branch-check-severity-status/.codex-runs/specled_-s65.8.log

Commit: 7ff0bbf69d1c65634d4427a3e7856c799f0e78e5
Files touched: .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.BranchCheckTest "emits finding when a new must requirement lacks a backing @tag spec" (updated); SpecLedEx.BranchCheckTest "attested-clean (file, subject) pair downgrades to :info with binding-naming message" (updated)
Verification stubs flipped: none

Summary: BranchCheck now derives report status from the count of error-severity findings, so info and warning findings remain visible without failing the aggregate branch report. I added a branch_guard requirement and status assertions for the warning-only new-requirement finding and the info-only attested-clean file-touch case; existing error-present cases still fail. I found no branch_guard or spec_drift_trailer scenario requiring warning-only branch findings to make mix spec.check exit non-zero; only scenarios with error-severity findings require non-zero exit.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4 (.spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (verifier.ex, trailer.ex, test_helper.exs, config.yml, spec_drift_trailer.spec.md all untouched; test_helper.exs specifically confirmed absent from diff)

Semantic checks:
- Status computation fix confirmed: yes. BEFORE: `"status" => if(findings == [], do: "pass", else: "fail")`. AFTER: `errors = Enum.count(findings, &(&1["severity"] == "error"))` then `"status" => if(errors > 0, do: "fail", else: "pass")` (branch_check.ex:118,123). Mirrors verifier.ex:95/103 (`errors = Enum.count(findings, &(&1["severity"] == "error"))` -> `"status" => if(fail?, do: "fail", else: "pass")`). Correctly omits the strict?/warnings term since branch_check has no strict flag.
- Warning-severity behavior claim independently verified: yes. Read every scenario naming a :warning finding and every "exits non-zero" assertion. All 5 non-zero-exit scenarios in branch_guard.spec.md have an error-severity finding present: enforcement_error_escalates_finding (severity error via enforcement:error), file_touch_drift_no_relaxation (missing_subject_update at error), file_touch_surface_only_unbound_stays_strict (error), file_touch_multi_subject_partial_attestation ("because of subj_b" at error), file_touch_severity_pin_overrides_attestation (error). The two warning scenarios (new_requirement_without_tag) explicitly assert status stays PASS. spec_drift_trailer.spec.md scenarios assert only severity resolution, no exit codes. triangulation.spec.md scenarios for `branch_guard_untested_realization` (the config warning default) assert emission/suppression, never exit codes. No scenario requires a warning-only finding to fail spec.check. Claim holds.
- Requirements coverage:
  - specled.branch_guard.status_follows_error_severity (new must): covered by branch_check_test.exs:71 (warning-only finding -> status "pass") and :490 (info-only attested-clean -> status "pass"). Both assert report["status"], not just finding severity (the exact gap the bug shipped through). Would fail under the old finding-count logic, which marked any nonempty findings list "fail".
  - error-present still fails: branch_check_test.exs:231 (enforcement-escalated error -> status "fail") and :314 (append_only/requirement_deleted error -> status "fail"). Would fail if error findings stopped counting.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Only the aggregate status derivation changed; no change to Severity.resolve, trailer parsing, or finding generation. The new `errors` binding is used solely in the status ternary.
- Project verification: pass. mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix format --check-formatted clean; mix deps.unlock --check-unused clean; mix test = 816 tests, 2 properties, 0 failures (one benign "specled_cmd_*.sh: No such file" temp-script cleanup race under heavy concurrent host load, not a test failure); mix spec.check exit 0, status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0, and the new requirement's tagged_tests verification reached "executed" strength. Live demonstration on the branch: the sole branch finding is branch_guard_missing_subject_update at severity info -> status pass -> exit 0, matching file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only): The fix omits any warning term (unlike verifier.ex which fails on warnings under strict?), which is correct today because branch_check has no strict flag and no scenario requires warning-only exit failure. If a strict mode is ever added to branch_check, revisit whether warnings should fail there. The new requirement is stability:evolving, appropriate for a still-settling behavior.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub coverage, full requirement coverage,
zero mechanical or intent-level scope violations, and passing project verification.
No auditor was spawned per selective-audit policy.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-s65/realization-enabled-tiers. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: Fix branch_check status/exit code to be severity-aware (info/warning findings incorrectly fail mix spec.check)
